parsed_review.summary: CP00-281 is a clean descriptor-only closeout pack (Risk C, 150 units, RP08.P02.M09.S09-RP08.P03.M06.S12) closing the RP08.P02 service phase tail and opening the RP08.P03 interface foundation. Independent read-only inspection (Read/Grep/Glob) confirms full internal consistency across closeout-pack-plan.json, contracts/email-dms-core-contract.json, registry.js, service.js, validators.js, model.test.js, validate-rp08-email-dms-core-contract.mjs, and README.md. Pack identity, deliverable distribution (ui6/impl63/claude_review6/failure_recovery4/test20/contract31/security_audit16/hermes_evidence4=150), phase counts (P02:34, P03:116), micro-title counts, and per-section row titles match the baseline exactly and are enforced by validateEmailDmsCoreCp281Coverage. Descriptor-only and no-write/no-real-data boundaries are comprehensive: freezeCp281Result and EMAIL_DMS_CORE_CP281_NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION set every runtime/persistence/real-data flag false, the no-leak guards are enforced row-by-row, and the in-scope source modules are pure frozen-object construction with no fs/network/child_process; the validator script is read-only (node:fs/promises readFile + assert). Authority boundary is correct: claude_is_final_approval=false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening=true, promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false, claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation=false; the Claude packet is read_only with tools [Read,Grep,Glob] and carries the forbidden-review-evidence blockers. The pack does not overclaim production_ready: command-evidence.json records production_ready=false with gate_outcome implementation_gates_passed_post_review_closeout_pending, and production_ready_candidate=true is correctly scoped to passing coverage+foundation validation. The Hermes packet is a deterministic descriptor receipt (gate H08, emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false), not embedded product runtime. Review-hardening inputs are valid: readiness auth-preflight passed (exit 0, 1434-byte valid-JSON stdout), and validation-report confirms allowed-tools-only with source_inspection_mode read_tools_used. Untracked dirty files (the .docx, new-session-handoff.md, cp00-281 docs dir, and the LDIP invalid-attempt JSON) are correctly classified as unrelated and not attributed to this pack. No P0/P1/P2/P3 findings. Final enterprise-trust closure remains a human responsibility outside this read-only review.
